_meta.description: Mapping of finding types to Cyber Assessment Framework (CAF) outcomes. CAF is the UK National Cyber Security Centre's outcomes-based framework used by regulators. Objective B (Protecting Against Cyber Attack) is where most cloud misconfigurations land.
_meta.mapping_rationale: CAF is outcomes-based, not control-based. Each outcome describes a state the organisation should reach; mappings link finding types to the outcome(s) they most directly compromise. Objective B outcomes are chosen for cloud misconfigurations because Objective B covers Protecting Against Cyber Attack, which is where technical misconfigurations sit.

audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_VIA_ACL: Two references. B2.a is primary — a bucket readable by AllUsers has bypassed identity verification and authorisation entirely. B3.a is secondary and softer — 'Understanding Data' captures the case where the organisation may not know that the misconfigured bucket contains sensitive data. B3.a is defensible but weaker than B2.a.
audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_ACCESS_BLOCK_DISABLED: B4.a covers 'Secure by design'. PAB is the security-by-default configuration for S3 buckets; leaving it disabled is a design failure. This mapping is clean.
audit_notes.S3_ENCRYPTION_DISABLED: B3.c covers 'Stored data' outcomes and explicitly includes encryption at rest as a control. This mapping is unambiguous.
